component.name: finbreak
component.summary: Understand your personal finances, privately and offline

description.p: finbreak is a private, offline desktop app for understanding your money. Import bank statements (CSV, OFX, or PDF), categorise transactions, and see where your money goes — with everything stored locally in an encrypted vault that only your password can open.

ul.li: Import statements from CSV, OFX, and PDF, with per-bank mappings
Automatic and rule-based transaction categorisation you can override
A spending dashboard with drill-down by category and merchant
Password-protected PDF reports and encrypted backups
Strong local encryption (SQLCipher AES-256, Argon2id key)
No accounts, no cloud, no tracking — your data never leaves your device

screenshot.caption: See where your money goes at a glance
Organise categories up to three levels deep
Spot recurring money — subscriptions, debit orders and salary
Transfers between your own accounts, kept out of spending
Auto-sort transactions with your own rules

ul.li: Flatpak and native Linux packages (RPM + deb) — install finbreak via GNOME Software, KDE Discover, or your distro's package manager with a bundled, tested runtime.
Three-level category organization (Type › Category › Sub-category) with full rename, move and delete support at every level.
Report an Issue menu item; signed SHA256SUMS checksums and per-platform software-component lists (SBOMs) for independent release verification.
Password-safety features: vault reset for forgotten passwords and a Settings button to verify a backup is readable before restore.
Amounts display currency symbols correctly ("R 1,234.49") with a dedicated Currency column; confirmed transfers now show directionally on the Transactions tab.
Optional password hint on the unlock screen, stored unencrypted and readable before unlock as a memory aid (keep it vague, not your password).
Copy a transaction's amount or description with automatic clipboard clear after a configurable timeout (default 30 seconds) so sensitive data doesn't linger.
Unlock delays for repeated wrong password attempts — each retry gets slower (1s, then 2s, 4s, up to 30s) to defend against guessing attacks.
OFX import now files each transaction under the date the bank printed, even when the statement carries a timezone.
The delete-statement confirmation no longer over-states the loss when the statement overlaps another one you keep.
Deleting a statement no longer loses transactions that an overlapping statement still covers — they are handed over to the statement you keep.
